purpose: End-to-end smoke tests that run the full ultrainit pipeline against a minimal synthetic Flask project using real Claude API calls. Unlike unit/integration tests (which mock Claude), these validate the complete bundled artifact, actual model output quality, cost tracking, and generated artifact usability — catching integration failures that mocks cannot detect.

architecture.overview: Two-layer design: a single orchestrator script (run-smoke.sh) that drives the full pipeline, and a 4-file synthetic mini-project that serves as the analysis target. The smoke test is intentionally not integrated into automated CI (costs real money) — it runs manually or at release time. It has three execution modes (bundled/source/curl) to test different distribution paths. Assertions are 12 hand-written checks using grep/jq directly against the generated artifacts rather than bats-style assertions.

subdirectories.purpose: Root — contains only run-smoke.sh, the test orchestrator

Synthetic target codebase: a 4-file minimal Flask TODO REST API designed to be small enough to analyze in seconds but rich enough to exercise all gathering agents (Python language, Flask framework, pytest suite, README)

architecture.data_flow: run-smoke.sh cleans prior artifacts → selects execution mode (bundled/source/curl) → runs ultrainit with --non-interactive --skip-research --budget 15 --model haiku against mini-project/ → waits for full pipeline completion → validates generated artifacts: CLAUDE.md (line count ≥50, project-specific terms, code blocks, no generic filler), .claude/skills/ (≥1 skill), .ultrainit/findings/ (≥5 JSON files), .ultrainit/costs/ (≥1 cost file) → reports PASS/FAIL per check and exits 1 on any failure

key_files.purpose: Main smoke test orchestrator (161 lines). Handles 3 execution modes, cleans stale artifacts before each run, invokes ultrainit, and runs 12 artifact assertions. Uses FAILURES counter pattern — all checks run even if early ones fail, then exits 1 if any failed.

34-line Flask TODO API with 3 endpoints (GET/POST/DELETE). Chosen to give the identity, commands, patterns, and tooling agents enough signal to generate non-trivial output while keeping token consumption and runtime minimal.

4 pytest test cases exercising the Flask API. Presence of a test suite is required for the commands agent to find a 'test' command and for CLAUDE.md to include pytest references — one of the smoke test assertions checks for 'pytest' in CLAUDE.md.
Declares flask==3.0.0 and pytest==8.0.0. The identity agent reads this to determine frameworks and versions; the smoke test asserts CLAUDE.md mentions 'flask' and 'python'.
Minimal 4-section README (Setup, Testing, API). Feeds the docs-scanner agent and ensures the generated CLAUDE.md doesn't duplicate it verbatim.

patterns.name: FAILURES counter assertion pattern
patterns.description: All 12 checks run unconditionally; each failed check increments a FAILURES counter and prints a red message. Script exits 1 only at the very end if FAILURES > 0. This is in contrast to set -e fail-fast — the goal is to see ALL failures in one run rather than stopping at the first.

patterns.name: Three-mode execution switch
patterns.description: run-smoke.sh parses --source and --curl flags to select one of three invocation strategies: (1) default 'bundled' — runs dist/ultrainit.sh (calls make bundle if missing); (2) --source — runs ultrainit.sh directly from repo root; (3) --curl — simulates curl-pipe-bash using local bundle. This tests all three distribution paths without duplication of assertion logic.

patterns.name: Content-specific assertions (anti-generic check)
patterns.description: The smoke test asserts that CLAUDE.md contains project-specific strings ('flask', 'python', 'todo', 'api') AND does NOT contain generic filler phrases ('best practice', 'clean code', 'SOLID principles'). This is a proxy for model output quality — it catches regressions where the synthesizer produces boilerplate instead of codebase-specific guidance.

patterns.name: Artifact existence + minimum-length checks
patterns.description: Rather than parsing generated content structurally, smoke tests use line-count thresholds (CLAUDE.md ≥50 lines), directory non-empty checks (.claude/skills/ has ≥1 file), and file count checks (.ultrainit/findings/ has ≥5 JSON files). These are deliberately loose — enough to detect total failures without being brittle to cosmetic output changes.

patterns.name: Pre-run artifact cleanup
patterns.description: run-smoke.sh always deletes .ultrainit/, CLAUDE.md, and .claude/ from mini-project/ before running. This ensures each smoke run starts from a clean slate — no stale cached findings from a previous run can mask a real failure (the --force flag is implied).

conventions.naming: Single orchestrator script named run-smoke.sh (verb-noun, kebab-case). Mini-project follows Flask conventions (app.py, tests/test_app.py). No bats file naming — smoke tests are plain bash, not bats-core.
conventions.imports: run-smoke.sh sources no helpers — it is self-contained. It does not use test_helper.bash or mock_claude.bash (those are for bats unit tests). All dependencies are standard Unix tools: grep, jq, wc, find, bash.
conventions.error_handling: FAILURES counter pattern (described above). Each check is a standalone if/then block; failed checks print a red [FAIL] line and increment FAILURES. At the end: 'if [[ $FAILURES -gt 0 ]]; then exit 1; fi'. No trap handlers.
other: Color output: Uses ANSI codes (GREEN=\033[0;32m, RED=\033[0;31m, NC=\033[0m) for terminal readability. Not disabled in smoke tests (unlike unit tests which suppress color via NO_COLOR).
Budget cap: Always passes --budget 15 to limit runaway costs. Combined with --model haiku and --skip-research, keeps smoke runs under $2.
No bats dependency: Smoke tests can run on any system with bash + jq + the claude CLI. They do NOT require Docker or bats-core installation.

internal: dist/ultrainit.sh (bundled mode) — built by 'make bundle' from ultrainit.sh + lib/ + schemas/ + prompts/ + scripts/
ultrainit.sh (source mode) — the unbundled entry point
lib/ — all phase libraries, indirectly exercised through ultrainit.sh
schemas/ — JSON schemas used by agents during the run
prompts/ — agent system prompts used during the run

external_key.name: claude CLI
external_key.purpose: Real Claude API calls — smoke tests make genuine claude -p invocations, unlike unit tests which mock it

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: Validates settings.json is parseable JSON (smoke assertion #3) and processes cost files

external_key.name: grep
external_key.purpose: Content assertions on CLAUDE.md (term presence, generic phrase absence)

external_key.name: bash
external_key.purpose: Script runtime — no bats-core required for smoke tests

external_key.name: make
external_key.purpose: run-smoke.sh calls 'make bundle' automatically if dist/ultrainit.sh is missing

domain_terms.meaning: End-to-end test using real Claude API against a synthetic mini-project; validates integration, not just logic. Costs real money (~$1–$2 per run). Not run in CI.
Default smoke test mode: tests dist/ultrainit.sh, the self-extracting single-file release artifact. Tests that bundling didn't break anything.
Smoke test mode (--source flag): runs ultrainit.sh directly from repo root without bundling. Faster for development iteration.
Smoke test mode (--curl flag): simulates the curl-pipe-bash installation path using the local bundle as a stand-in for the download.
The 4-file synthetic Flask TODO API in tests/smoke/mini-project/ that serves as the analysis target for smoke tests.
Integer variable in run-smoke.sh that accumulates failed check count. All checks run before the script exits, enabling visibility into all failures at once.
Smoke assertion that verifies CLAUDE.md does NOT contain filler phrases like 'best practice' or 'clean code' — proxy for synthesizer output quality.

gotchas.issue: Smoke tests are NOT idempotent by default without pre-cleanup — re-running without cleanup would find cached .ultrainit/ findings and skip agents, potentially masking failures
gotchas.solution: run-smoke.sh explicitly deletes .ultrainit/, CLAUDE.md, and .claude/ before each run. Never remove this cleanup step. If adding new artifacts, add them to the cleanup block too.

gotchas.issue: Bundled mode calls 'make bundle' automatically if dist/ultrainit.sh is missing, but make must be available and the build must succeed. A build failure will cause the smoke test to fail with a cryptic error rather than a clear 'bundle failed' message.
gotchas.solution: Run 'make bundle' manually first if troubleshooting smoke test startup failures. Check dist/ exists before running smoke tests in environments without make.

gotchas.issue: Smoke tests use --skip-research but NOT --non-interactive — the ask.sh phase will try to read from /dev/tty. If run in a headless environment (no terminal), the ask phase will hang or fail.
gotchas.solution: run-smoke.sh passes --non-interactive. If modifying the invocation, always keep --non-interactive for headless/CI use.

gotchas.issue: The anti-generic phrase check is a grep against a hardcoded list ('best practice', 'clean code', 'SOLID principles'). If the synthesizer prompt changes and starts using different generic phrases, this check won't catch them.
gotchas.solution: When updating the synthesizer prompt or output format, review and extend the hardcoded phrase list in run-smoke.sh. The list is not derived from the validate.sh production validator — it's a separate, manually maintained set.

gotchas.issue: Smoke tests do NOT run integration tests (bats). They are separate systems with separate dependencies. A developer might fix a unit test failure but not realize the smoke test still fails because bundling is broken.
gotchas.solution: After any change to the bundling logic (Makefile, ultrainit.sh header/footer), always run 'make smoke' in addition to 'make test-all'.

gotchas.issue: The line count threshold for CLAUDE.md is only 50 lines — much lower than the production validator's 100-line minimum. A seriously degraded model output could still pass the smoke test while failing production validation.
gotchas.solution: The thresholds are intentionally low to avoid false failures from model output variance. The production validator (Phase 5) enforces stricter rules. Smoke tests are a coarse sanity check, not a quality gate.

commands.purpose: Run smoke test using bundled dist/ultrainit.sh (builds bundle if missing)
Run smoke test using unbundled ultrainit.sh directly (faster for dev)
Run smoke test simulating curl-pipe-bash installation path
Makefile shorthand for the bundled smoke test
Makefile shorthand for the source-mode smoke test
Build dist/ultrainit.sh before running bundled smoke tests

skill_opportunities.name: run-smoke-test
skill_opportunities.description: Trigger a smoke test run in the correct mode, check output for failures, and summarize results. Useful when a developer needs to validate a change end-to-end before opening a PR.
workflow_steps: If bundled, verify dist/ultrainit.sh exists or run 'make bundle'
Report pass/fail count and highlight failed checks

skill_opportunities.name: add-smoke-assertion
skill_opportunities.description: Add a new assertion to run-smoke.sh following the FAILURES counter pattern. Useful when new artifact types are added to ultrainit's output.
workflow_steps: Read tests/smoke/run-smoke.sh to understand current assertion format
Identify what artifact or property needs validation
Write assertion using if/grep or if/jq pattern with FAILURES increment
Add cleanup of new artifact to the pre-run cleanup block
